name: Thirdweb
what_it_does: Web3 development platform that enables AI agents to deploy smart contracts, mint NFTs, authenticate wallets, and execute gasless blockchain transactions via REST API and TypeScript/Python SDKs.
use_cases: Build an agent that mints NFT rewards when users complete tasks, using gasless relayer to hide gas complexity
Authenticate users to a web3 application by verifying wallet signatures via Thirdweb Auth without managing keys
Deploy and interact with ERC-20 or ERC-721 contracts programmatically without writing raw Solidity deployment scripts
Create a marketplace agent that lists, buys, and transfers NFTs on behalf of users using sponsored transactions
Query on-chain contract state and transaction history across EVM chains from a single unified API
not_for: Non-EVM blockchains (Solana, Bitcoin) — Thirdweb is EVM-focused
High-frequency trading or MEV strategies requiring sub-millisecond transaction submission
Agents that need to manage private keys directly — use a dedicated key management service for that
best_when: You want to add blockchain functionality to an agent without deep Solidity knowledge, using pre-built contract templates and a managed infrastructure layer.
avoid_when: You need custom low-level EVM control, non-EVM chain support, or want to avoid third-party infrastructure between your agent and the chain.

auth.notes: Client ID for frontend/read operations; Secret Key for backend/write operations. Keep Secret Key server-side only.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Generous free tier with API calls, contract deployments, and dashboard access
pricing.paid_tiers: Growth and Pro tiers for higher throughput, Engine hosting, and SLA guarantees
pricing.notes: Blockchain gas fees are separate from Thirdweb platform fees. Gasless relayer is a paid add-on.

performance.rate_limits: Free: 1000 requests/sec per API key; paid tiers higher; Engine self-hosted removes limits

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST errors include chain-specific revert reasons when available; gas estimation failures return actionable messages.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Read operations are idempotent; write operations (transactions) are not — duplicate submissions result in duplicate on-chain transactions.
known_agent_gotchas: Transaction hashes must be polled for confirmation — do not assume finality immediately after submission
Secret Key vs. Client ID confusion is a common auth mistake — read-only endpoints accept Client ID but writes require Secret Key
Gasless transactions require a relayer setup and funded relayer wallet — agents must monitor relayer balance
Contract deployments are chain-specific — deploying to testnet vs. mainnet requires explicit chain ID configuration
ERC-721 vs. ERC-1155 distinctions affect minting API parameters — using the wrong contract type returns confusing errors

security_score_components.security_notes: Secret Key exposure in client-side code is a critical risk; Thirdweb Engine is recommended for server-side key management at scale.
